nodes.context: Iraqi Shia Islamist political party and paramilitary organization, formerly the military arm of SCIRI.

nodes.standard_name: Supreme Council for Islamic Revolution in Iraq
nodes.original_text_name: Supreme Council for Islamic Revolution in Iraq
nodes.context: Shia Islamic party based in Iran, whose military arm was the Badr Brigade.

nodes.standard_name: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant
nodes.original_text_name: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant
nodes.context: Jihadist militant group that Badr Organization fought against in Iraq.
Coalition of Iraqi militias, of which Badr Organization is a part.
Shia militia led by Muqtada al-Sadr, accused of sectarian killings alongside Badr Brigade.
Lebanese Shia militant group, compared to Badr Organization in role.

edges.target: Supreme Council for Islamic Revolution in Iraq
edges.evidence_quote: The Badr Brigade, formed in 1982 and led by Iranian officers, served as the military arm of the Supreme Council for Islamic Revolution in Iraq (SCIRI)
edges.context: Badr Organization was the military wing of SCIRI.

edges.target: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant
edges.evidence_quote: Badr Brigade forces, and their Iranian commanders, have come to prominence in 2014 fighting the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) in Iraq.
edges.context: Badr Organization fought against ISIL in Iraq.

edges.evidence_quote: It is a part of the Popular Mobilization Forces.
edges.context: Badr Organization is a component of the Popular Mobilization Forces.

edges.evidence_quote: Another was the Mahdi Army of the young cleric Muqtada al-Sadr, who is now part of the Shia coalition seeking to form a government after winning the mid-December election.
edges.context: Both Badr Brigade and Mahdi Army were accused of carrying out sectarian killings.
